A CloudNative Inc esta no projeto microservices-refactor e precisa migrar database views. Como proceder no staging (us-phoenix-1)? [context: shape=VM.Standard3.Flex, 4OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#123]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para onprem-database - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "onprem-database-staging-122" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='AVAILABLE'].{name:"display-name",db:"db-name"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database update \
  --autonomous-database-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database get \
  --autonomous-database-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci db autonomous-database delete \
  --autonomous-database-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `200 GB`

- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico

6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on Oracle to Autonomous Database migration.
Quais sao os requisitos para migrar database indexes no ambiente da CloudNative Inc (microservices-refactor)? [context: shape=BM.Standard.E5, 48OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#103]

**Migration Guide — On-premises → OCI (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (On-premises → OCI):**
| On-premises | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migracao:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs On-premises

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicacoes (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em metricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- Otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migracao OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: Migracao direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: Automacao de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm

Como configurar data quality monitoring com foco em configurar para FoodChain Trace no projeto database-consolidation? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 32OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#38]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para data-transfer - FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci os bucket create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "data-transfer-data-platform-037" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci os bucket list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",tier:"storage-tier"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci os bucket update \
  --bucket-name <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci os bucket get \
  --bucket-name <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci os bucket delete \
  --bucket-name <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.A1.Flex`
- OCPUs: `32`
- Storage: `100 GB`

A TechCorp Brasil precisa configurar data transformation para o projeto ecommerce-migration. Como fazer no compartment production? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 16OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=10.10.0.0/16, ticket#101]

**Migration Guide — Cloud provider → OCI (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Mapeamento de servicos (Cloud provider → OCI):**
| Cloud provider | OCI | CLI Command |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| VM instances | OCI Compute | `oci compute instance launch` |
| Cloud storage | Object Storage | `oci os object put` |
| Managed database | Autonomous Database | `oci db autonomous-database create` |

**Fases da migracao:**

**Fase 1: Assessment (1-2 semanas)**
- Inventariar workloads e dependencias
- Classificar por criticidade e complexidade
- Definir estrategia: rehost, replatform, refactor
- Estimar custos OCI vs Cloud provider

**Fase 2: Foundation (1-2 semanas)**
- Criar VCN, subnets, gateways
- Configurar IAM, compartments, policies
- Estabelecer conectividade (IPSec/FastConnect)
- Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migration (2-4 semanas)**
- Migrar dados primeiro (Database Migration Service)
- Migrar aplicacoes (lift-and-shift ou replatform)
- Validar funcionalidade e performance
- Executar cutover com minimo downtime

**Fase 4: Optimization (continuo)**
- Right-size resources baseado em metricas reais
- Implementar auto-scaling
- Otimizar custos com Universal Credits
- Documentar runbooks OCI

**Ferramentas de migracao OCI:**
- **Database Migration Service**: Online migration com minimo downtime
- **Data Transfer Service**: Appliance fisico para grandes volumes
- **Object Storage Migration Service**: Migracao direta entre clouds
- **VMware Solution**: Lift-and-shift de workloads VMware
- **oci-cli**: Automacao de provisioning

**Checklist de cutover:**
- [ ] DNS atualizado para endpoints OCI
- [ ] SSL/TLS certificates instalados
- [ ] Health checks passando
- [ ] Monitoring e alertas ativos
- [ ] Backup configurado
- [ ] Runbook de rollback preparado

**Referencia:** [OCI Migration Guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/migration.htm)

Como implementar data quality monitoring com configurar para a AgriTech Innovations no projeto hybrid-cloud-integration (devops, me-jeddah-1)? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 2OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.1.0.0/16, ticket#90]

**Disaster Recovery — Data Transfer (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicacao
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperacao
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicacao e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

Qual a melhor abordagem para configurar best practices seguranca no contexto da LogiTrack Logistics projeto security-hardening? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 64OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#128]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para provider - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "provider-data-platform-127" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`

**Security Audit — Provider (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

A CloudNative Inc precisa configurar instance principal authentication para o projeto microservices-refactor. Como fazer no compartment staging? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 4OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#3]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para provider - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "provider-staging-002" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `200 GB`

Melhores praticas para diagnosticar instance provisioning failures no contexto FoodChain Trace/database-consolidation (data-platform, ca-toronto-1) [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 2OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.1.0.0/16, ticket#138]

**Performance Tuning — Compute (FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation)**

**Metodologia de benchmark:**
1. Establish baseline com metricas atuais
2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimizacao isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: metricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

Descreva o processo de diagnosticar instance clone problems para BioResearch Labs (api-gateway-deployment) em shared-services/uk-london-1 [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 48OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#135]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para compute - BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='shared-services'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "compute-shared-services-134" \
  --region uk-london-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "shared-services", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `shared-services`
- Region: `uk-london-1`
- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `200 GB`

**Disaster Recovery — Storage (HealthTech Systems - cost-optimization)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicacao
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperacao
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicacao e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

**Security Audit — Networking (LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

A RetailMax Online precisa gerenciar networking para o projeto compliance-audit. Como fazer no compartment shared-services? [context: shape=VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex, 16OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.10.0.0/16, ticket#45]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para networking - RetailMax Online - compliance-audit:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci network vcn create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='shared-services'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "networking-shared-services-044" \
  --region uk-london-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci network vcn list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='AVAILABLE'].{name:"display-name",cidr:"cidr-block"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci network vcn update \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "RetailMax Online - compliance-audit"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "shared-services", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci network vcn get \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci network vcn delete \
  --vcn-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `shared-services`
- Region: `uk-london-1`
- Shape: `VM.DenseIO.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `16`
- Storage: `200 GB`

**Integration Pattern — Container (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Servicos OCI que integram com container:**
1. **OCI Vault**: Encryption keys e secrets management
2. **OCI Monitoring**: Metricas e alertas
3. **OCI Logging**: Logs estruturados
4. **OCI IAM**: Politicas de acesso
5. **OCI Notifications**: Alertas e eventos

**Padrao comum:**
```
Recurso OCI → Events → Service Connector Hub → Destination
                                    ↓
                        ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                        ↓           ↓           ↓
                  Functions   Notifications  Streaming
```

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to manage objects in compartment devops
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to use secret-family in compartment devops
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

Melhores praticas para resolver acesso bloqueado no contexto InsureTech Plus/identity-federation (devops, me-jeddah-1) [context: shape=BM.Standard.E5, 64OCPUs, 500GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#40]

**Security Audit — Security (InsureTech Plus - identity-federation)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

Quais etapas para configurar Security List para bastion host da SmartCity IoT (projeto backup-strategy, compartment staging)? [context: shape=BM.Standard.E5, 16OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.10.0.0/16, ticket#13]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para security - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci vault secret create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "security-staging-012" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci vault secret list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"secret-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci <service> update \
  --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci vault secret get \
  --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci vault secret schedule-secret-deletion \
  --secret-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `BM.Standard.E5`
- OCPUs: `16`
- Storage: `50 GB`

Implementacao via OCI CLI para observability - FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "observability-data-platform-017" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"is-enabled"=='true'].{name:"display-name",metric:"metric-compartment-id"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm update \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm get \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm delete \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

Como planejar e executar gerenciar observability para AgriTech Innovations no projeto hybrid-cloud-integration (me-jeddah-1)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E3.Flex, 32OCPUs, 500GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#70]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Observability (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id devops \
  --display-name "high-cpu-devops" \
  --metric-compartment-id devops \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Descreva o processo de gerenciar observability para CloudNative Inc (microservices-refactor) em staging/us-phoenix-1 [context: shape=VM.GPU.A10.1, 48OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#23]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para observability - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "observability-staging-022" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"is-enabled"=='true'].{name:"display-name",metric:"metric-compartment-id"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm update \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm get \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci monitoring alarm delete \
  --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.GPU.A10.1`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `1000 GB`

Como resolver problemas de devops para CloudNative Inc (microservices-refactor) no compartment staging (us-phoenix-1)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E3.Flex, 4OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#43]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para devops - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci devops project create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "devops-staging-042" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci devops project list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci devops project update \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci devops project get \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci devops project delete \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `4`
- Storage: `50 GB`

**Integration Pattern — DevOps (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**CI/CD Pipeline:**
```
Git Repo → OCI DevOps Build → OCIR → OKE / Compute
                ↓
        OCI DevOps Deploy
                ↓
        OCI Functions / Compute Instance Group
                ↓
        OCI Monitoring (health check)
```

**Infrastructure as Code:**
- Terraform via OCI Resource Manager
- State management em Object Storage
- Plans automaticos em MRs
- Apply via pipeline aprovado

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'shared-services'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'shared-services'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group shared-services-functions to manage objects in compartment shared-services
Allow dynamic-group shared-services-functions to use secret-family in compartment shared-services
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

**Security Audit — State (RetailMax Online - compliance-audit)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

**Disaster Recovery — State (FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicacao
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperacao
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicacao e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

**Disaster Recovery — State (AutoDrive Systems - container-platform)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicacao
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperacao
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicacao e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

A equipe da EduPlatform Global quer configurar log export Object Storage para performance-tuning. Qual configuracao no compartment security? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E3.Flex, 48OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#7]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Logging (EduPlatform Global - performance-tuning)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id security \
  --display-name "high-cpu-security" \
  --metric-compartment-id security \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Logging/overview.htm

Implementacao via OCI CLI para logging - TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci logging log-group create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='development'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "logging-development-051" \
  --region us-ashburn-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci logging log-group list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci <service> update \
  --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "development", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci logging log-group get \
  --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci logging log-group delete \
  --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `development`
- Region: `us-ashburn-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `8`
- Storage: `500 GB`

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para logging - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci logging log-group get --log-group-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `staging`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

Quais etapas para configurar Monitoring compliance da EnergyGrid Monitor (projeto serverless-transformation, compartment security)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E3.Flex, 1OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.0.0.0/16, ticket#97]

**Integration Pattern — Observability (EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation)**

**Arquitetura de Observabilidade:**
```
Applications → OCI Logging ──┐
                             ↓
Infrastructure → OCI Monitoring → Service Connector Hub
                             ↓
User Experience → OCI APM ───┘
            ↓
    OCI Notifications
            ↓
    PagerDuty / Slack / Email
```

**Service Connector Hub flows:**
- Logs → Object Storage (compliance archive)
- Logs → Functions (real-time processing)
- Metrics → Notifications (alerting)
- Audit → Streaming (security analysis)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'security'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'security'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group security-functions to manage objects in compartment security
Allow dynamic-group security-functions to use secret-family in compartment security
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

Qual a melhor abordagem para instalar Monitoring agents no contexto da LogiTrack Logistics projeto security-hardening? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 64OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#128]

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para monitoring - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci monitoring alarm get --alarm-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `data-platform`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

Quais etapas para configurar Stack Monitoring plugins da CloudNative Inc (projeto microservices-refactor, compartment staging)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 4OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#83]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Stack Monitoring (CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id staging \
  --display-name "high-cpu-staging" \
  --metric-compartment-id staging \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para stack-monitoring - HealthTech Systems - cost-optimization:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci stack-monitoring target get --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `networking`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

Como configurar database monitoring para BioResearch Labs (api-gateway-deployment) no compartment shared-services (uk-london-1)? [context: shape=VM.Standard3.Flex, 48OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#15]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para stack-monitoring - BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='shared-services'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "stack-monitoring-shared-services-014" \
  --region uk-london-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",type:"resource-type"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target update \
  --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "BioResearch Labs - api-gateway-deployment"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "shared-services", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target get \
  --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci stack-monitoring target delete \
  --target-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `shared-services`
- Region: `uk-london-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `200 GB`

**Integration Pattern — Observability (TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul)**

**Arquitetura de Observabilidade:**
```
Applications → OCI Logging ──┐
                             ↓
Infrastructure → OCI Monitoring → Service Connector Hub
                             ↓
User Experience → OCI APM ───┘
            ↓
    OCI Notifications
            ↓
    PagerDuty / Slack / Email
```

**Service Connector Hub flows:**
- Logs → Object Storage (compliance archive)
- Logs → Functions (real-time processing)
- Metrics → Notifications (alerting)
- Audit → Streaming (security analysis)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'development'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'development'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group development-functions to manage objects in compartment development
Allow dynamic-group development-functions to use secret-family in compartment development
```

2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimizacao isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: metricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

Best practices para apm - AutoDrive Systems - container-platform:

**1. Planejamento e Design**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de apm
- Escolha a regiao `ap-mumbai-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `networking` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**2. Implementacao**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: AutoDrive Systems - container-platform`
  - `environment: networking`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**3. Seguranca**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**4. Operacao**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**5. Otimizacao**

- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente

Melhores praticas para integrar APM com servicos OCI no contexto TravelHub Platform/monitoring-overhaul (development, us-ashburn-1) [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 8OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=192.168.0.0/16, ticket#12]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Apm (TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id development \
  --display-name "high-cpu-development" \
  --metric-compartment-id development \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/apm/overview.htm

**Integration Pattern — Observability (InsureTech Plus - identity-federation)**

**Arquitetura de Observabilidade:**
```
Applications → OCI Logging ──┐
                             ↓
Infrastructure → OCI Monitoring → Service Connector Hub
                             ↓
User Experience → OCI APM ───┘
            ↓
    OCI Notifications
            ↓
    PagerDuty / Slack / Email
```

**Service Connector Hub flows:**
- Logs → Object Storage (compliance archive)
- Logs → Functions (real-time processing)
- Metrics → Notifications (alerting)
- Audit → Streaming (security analysis)

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to manage objects in compartment devops
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to use secret-family in compartment devops
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

A SmartCity IoT precisa analisar latency APM para o projeto backup-strategy. Como fazer no compartment staging? [context: shape=VM.Standard.E4.Flex, 1OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.0.0.0/16, ticket#73]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para apm - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci apm domain create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "apm-staging-072" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci apm domain list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci apm domain update \
  --apm-domain-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci apm domain get \
  --apm-domain-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci apm domain delete \
  --apm-domain-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `1`
- Storage: `50 GB`

**Performance Tuning — Performance (EnergyGrid Monitor - serverless-transformation)**

**Metodologia de benchmark:**
1. Establish baseline com metricas atuais
2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimizacao isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: metricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

Qual a melhor abordagem para diagnosticar network latency problems no contexto da AgriTech Innovations projeto hybrid-cloud-integration? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 32OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#30]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Performance (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id devops \
  --display-name "high-cpu-devops" \
  --metric-compartment-id devops \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Como planejar e executar diagnosticar federation authentication failures para InsureTech Plus no projeto identity-federation (me-jeddah-1)? [context: shape=VM.GPU.A10.1, 8OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=192.168.0.0/16, ticket#140]

**Security Audit — Authentication (InsureTech Plus - identity-federation)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

**Referencias:**
- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

Qual a melhor abordagem para diagnosticar API key problems no contexto da AgriTech Innovations projeto hybrid-cloud-integration? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 32OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#30]

**Security Audit — Authentication (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Checklist de Seguranca:**

**1. Identity & Access Management**
- [ ] Policies com principio de menor privilegio
- [ ] Grupos IAM organizados por funcao (nao por pessoa)
- [ ] MFA habilitado para todos os usuarios
- [ ] API keys rotacionadas a cada 90 dias
- [ ] Dynamic groups para workloads (nao user credentials)

**2. Network Security**
- [ ] Security lists restritivas (deny by default)
- [ ] Network Security Groups por workload
- [ ] WAF habilitado para aplicacoes web publicas
- [ ] Private endpoints para servicos OCI
- [ ] Sem subnets publicas com acesso direto a databases

**3. Data Protection**
- [ ] Encryption em repouso (OCI Vault com chaves gerenciadas pelo cliente)
- [ ] Encryption em transito (TLS 1.2+)
- [ ] Backups automatizados e testados
- [ ] Cross-region replication para DR
- [ ] Object Storage com versionamento habilitado

**4. Monitoring & Compliance**
- [ ] OCI Cloud Guard habilitado
- [ ] Audit log ativado em todos os compartments
- [ ] Alarms para atividades suspeitas
- [ ] Revisao trimestral de acesso
- [ ] Compliance com regulamentacoes aplicaveis

**Ferramentas de verificacao:**
- OCI Security Zones (bloqueia configuracoes inseguras)
- OCI Cloud Guard (detecta misconfigurations automaticamente)
- OCI Vulner Scanning (imagens de container e hosts)
- OCI Configuration Auditor (regras CIS Benchmark)

- [OCI Security Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Security/Concepts/security.htm)
- [CIS Oracle Cloud Foundations Benchmark](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/oracle_cloud)

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para authentication - SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci iam user get --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

Quais etapas para diagnosticar certificate authentication problems da SmartCity IoT (projeto backup-strategy, compartment staging)? [context: shape=BM.Standard.E5, 16OCPUs, 50GB, AD=AD-1, CIDR=10.10.0.0/16, ticket#13]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para authentication - SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci iam compartment create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='staging'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "authentication-staging-012" \
  --region us-phoenix-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci iam compartment list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci iam user update \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SmartCity IoT - backup-strategy"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "staging", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci iam user get \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci iam user delete \
  --compartment-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `staging`
- Region: `us-phoenix-1`
- Shape: `BM.Standard.E5`
- OCPUs: `16`
- Storage: `50 GB`

**Disaster Recovery — Database (DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization)**

**Framework de DR:**
1. **Assess**: Identificar workloads criticos e RPO/RTO
2. **Protect**: Implementar backups e replicacao
3. **Recover**: Documentar runbooks de recuperacao
4. **Test**: Simular failover trimestralmente

**OCI DR Services:**
- OCI GoldenGate: replicacao de dados em tempo real
- OCI Data Guard: database standby automatico
- Cross-region replication: Object Storage, Block Volume
- Resource Manager: infraestrutura como codigo para rebuild

**Runbook de teste de DR:**
1. Notificar equipe de teste
2. Simular falha na regiao primaria
3. Executar failover para regiao secundaria
4. Validar aplicacao e dados
5. Failback para regiao primaria
6. Documentar licoes aprendidas

**Metricas de sucesso:**
- RPO real <= RPO planejado
- RTO real <= RTO planejado
- Zero data loss para workloads criticos

Como configurar instance lifecycle errors com foco em diagnosticar para HealthTech Systems no projeto cost-optimization? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 2OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.1.0.0/16, ticket#66]

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para compute - HealthTech Systems - cost-optimization:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci compute instance get --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `networking`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

Implementacao via OCI CLI para compute - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='data-platform'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "compute-data-platform-027" \
  --region ca-toronto-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci compute instance list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='RUNNING'].{name:"display-name",shape:"shape"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci compute instance update \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "data-platform", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci compute instance get \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci compute instance terminate \
  --instance-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

- Compartment: `data-platform`
- Region: `ca-toronto-1`
- Shape: `VM.Standard.E4.Flex`
- OCPUs: `8`

Orientacoes para diagnosticar cluster autoscaler problems no cenario da FoodChain Trace - projeto database-consolidation, ca-toronto-1, data-platform [context: shape=VM.Standard3.Flex, 32OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#78]

**Monitoring & Alerting — Oke (FoodChain Trace - database-consolidation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengoverview.htm

A SecureBank Corp esta no projeto ci-cd-pipeline e precisa diagnosticar cluster upgrade failures. Como proceder no production (sa-saopaulo-1)? [context: shape=VM.Standard.A1.Flex, 4OCPUs, 1000GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=172.16.0.0/16, ticket#11]

**Integration Pattern — Oke (SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline)**

**Servicos OCI que integram com oke:**
1. **OCI Vault**: Encryption keys e secrets management
2. **OCI Monitoring**: Metricas e alertas
3. **OCI Logging**: Logs estruturados
4. **OCI IAM**: Politicas de acesso
5. **OCI Notifications**: Alertas e eventos

**Padrao comum:**
```
Recurso OCI → Events → Service Connector Hub → Destination
                                    ↓
                        ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                        ↓           ↓           ↓
                  Functions   Notifications  Streaming
```

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to manage objects in compartment production
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to use secret-family in compartment production
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id data-platform \
  --display-name "high-cpu-data-platform" \
  --metric-compartment-id data-platform \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Qual a melhor abordagem para diagnosticar function memory limits no contexto da AgriTech Innovations projeto hybrid-cloud-integration? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 32OCPUs, 2000GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=10.20.0.0/16, ticket#30]

**Integration Pattern — Serverless (AgriTech Innovations - hybrid-cloud-integration)**

**Arquitetura:**
```
Internet → API Gateway → OCI Functions → OCI Services
                                    ↓
                    ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
                    ↓               ↓               ↓
              Object Storage   Autonomous DB   OCI Vault
```

**Fluxo de dados:**
1. Client faz request via API Gateway
2. API Gateway autentica e roteia para Function
3. Function processa, acessa servicos OCI via SDK
4. Response retornada ao client

**Exemplo de integracao:**
```python
import oci
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data: dict = None):
    object_storage = oci.object_storage.ObjectStorageClient(
        oci.config.from_file()
    )
    namespace = object_storage.get_namespace().data
    object_storage.put_object(
        namespace_name=namespace,
        bucket_name="results",
        object_name="output.json",
        put_object_body=oci.util.to_stream(data)
    )
    return response.Response(
        ctx, response_data={"status": "success"},
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )
```

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to manage objects in compartment devops
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to use secret-family in compartment devops
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

You are an OCI troubleshooting specialist with expertise in Functions. Provide diagnostic guidance on function invocation and API Gateway errors.

Como configurar function performance optimization com foco em diagnosticar para InsureTech Plus no projeto identity-federation? [context: shape=BM.GPU4.8, 64OCPUs, 100GB, AD=AD-2, CIDR=192.168.10.0/24, ticket#80]

**Integration Pattern — Serverless (InsureTech Plus - identity-federation)**

**Arquitetura:**
```
Internet → API Gateway → OCI Functions → OCI Services
                                    ↓
                    ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
                    ↓               ↓               ↓
              Object Storage   Autonomous DB   OCI Vault
```

**Fluxo de dados:**
1. Client faz request via API Gateway
2. API Gateway autentica e roteia para Function
3. Function processa, acessa servicos OCI via SDK
4. Response retornada ao client

**Exemplo de integracao:**
```python
import oci
from fdk import response

def handler(ctx, data: dict = None):
    object_storage = oci.object_storage.ObjectStorageClient(
        oci.config.from_file()
    )
    namespace = object_storage.get_namespace().data
    object_storage.put_object(
        namespace_name=namespace,
        bucket_name="results",
        object_name="output.json",
        put_object_body=oci.util.to_stream(data)
    )
    return response.Response(
        ctx, response_data={"status": "success"},
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    )
```

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'devops'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to manage objects in compartment devops
Allow dynamic-group devops-functions to use secret-family in compartment devops
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

```bash
  --display-name "ci-cd-analytics-038" \
  --region ap-sydney-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci devops project list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

```bash
oci devops project update \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "analytics", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci devops project get \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci devops project delete \
  --project-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

- Region: `ap-sydney-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`

Diagnostico e resolucao de problemas para resource-manager - FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup:

**Problema comum 1: Recurso nao inicia**
```bash
# Verificar estado do recurso
oci resource-manager stack get --stack-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', error:'fault-domain'}"

# Verificar eventos recentes
oci audit event list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?contains('request.resource.id', '<ocid>')]"
```

**Problema comum 2: Erro de permissao**
1. Verifique as IAM policies no compartment `sandbox`
2. Confirme que o usuario/grupo tem acesso ao servico
3. Verifique dynamic groups se usando instance principal

**Problema comum 3: Timeout de conexao**
1. Verifique Security Lists e NSGs
2. Confirme route tables e gateways
3. Teste conectividade com `nc` ou `telnet`

**Checklist de validacao:**
- [ ] Recurso esta no estado correto?
- [ ] Permissoes IAM configuradas?
- [ ] Networking configurado corretamente?
- [ ] Logs mostram erros?
- [ ] Metricas dentro do esperado?

**Integration Pattern — DevOps (SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline)**

**CI/CD Pipeline:**
```
Git Repo → OCI DevOps Build → OCIR → OKE / Compute
                ↓
        OCI DevOps Deploy
                ↓
        OCI Functions / Compute Instance Group
                ↓
        OCI Monitoring (health check)
```

**Infrastructure as Code:**
- Terraform via OCI Resource Manager
- State management em Object Storage
- Plans automaticos em MRs
- Apply via pipeline aprovado

**Dynamic Groups para integracao:**
```
ALL {resource.type = 'fnfunc', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
ALL {resource.type = 'compute', resource.compartment.id = 'production'}
```

**Policies necessarias:**
```
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to manage objects in compartment production
Allow dynamic-group production-functions to use secret-family in compartment production
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Integration Patterns](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/integration.htm)

Quais etapas para configurar stack disaster recovery da SecureBank Corp (projeto ci-cd-pipeline, compartment production)? [context: shape=VM.Optimized3.Flex, 48OCPUs, 200GB, AD=AD-3, CIDR=172.20.0.0/16, ticket#111]

Implementacao via OCI CLI para resource-manager - SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline:

**Criacao do recurso:**
```bash
oci resource-manager stack create \
  --compartment-id $(oci iam compartment list \
    --query "data[?name=='production'].id | [0]") \
  --display-name "resource-manager-production-110" \
  --region sa-saopaulo-1 \
  --wait-for-state AVAILABLE
```

**Listar recursos existentes:**
```bash
oci resource-manager stack list \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query "data[?"lifecycle-state"=='ACTIVE'].{name:"display-name",state:"lifecycle-state"}"
```

**Atualizar configuracao:**
```bash
oci resource-manager stack update \
  --stack-id <resource-ocid> \
  --defined-tags '{"project": {"name": "SecureBank Corp - ci-cd-pipeline"}}' \
  --freeform-tags '{"environment": "production", "managed-by": "cli"}'
```

**Monitoramento:**
```bash
oci resource-manager stack get \
  --stack-id <resource-ocid> \
  --query "data.{state:'lifecycle-state', time:'time-created'}"
```

**Remocao (com cuidado):**
```bash
oci resource-manager stack delete \
  --stack-id <resource-ocid> \
  --force
```

**Parametros utilizados:**
- Compartment: `production`
- Region: `sa-saopaulo-1`
- Shape: `VM.Optimized3.Flex`
- OCPUs: `48`
- Storage: `200 GB`
